title: Osyricera osyriceroides
intro: Osyricera osyriceroides, also known as Wild Guanabano or Anisopus thwaitesii, is a plant species within the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Osyricera osyriceroides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in the production of essential oils and ointments.
